title: Arrhenatherum parlatorei
taxonomy: Arrhenatherum parlatorei is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae, and the tribe Aveneae. The genus Arrhenatherum is closely related to Avena and is often included in the same subtribe, Stipeae. Further classification of A. parlatorei includes its rank as a species, native to the Mediterranean region. This species is often confused with Arrhenatherum elatius, with which it shares many characteristics.
uses: Arrhenatherum parlatorei is an endangered species and not much information is available about its medicinal uses. However, some sources suggest that certain types of oat grass, including the Arrhenatherum genus, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
